linting.notes: No linting tools configured. Shellcheck could be used for bash linting but is not currently set up. Scripts pass bash syntax validation (bash -n).

env_vars: GITHUB_TOKEN - Required: GitHub token for repository access
DOCKER_ENGINE - Optional: defaults to 'podman', can be 'docker'
testing.mock_patterns: Tests use real GitHub repositories for integration testing
testing.notes: test-entrypoint.bash is an integration test script that requires a GitHub token and downstream repository URL. It builds the Docker container, runs various test scenarios (branch-to-branch sync, release-following mode), and validates behavior against real GitHub repositories. Tests cannot run in isolation without GitHub credentials and access to test repositories.

ci.notes: This repository has no CI/CD configuration. It is a GitHub Action meant to be consumed by other repositories. No .github/workflows directory exists.

build.notes: Dockerfile uses registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi-minimal:9.3-1552 as base image. Installs bash and git via microdnf. Creates 'ci' user and copies entrypoint.sh with 555 permissions.

conventions.test_naming: Function-based tests with descriptive names (before_all, after_all)
conventions.notes: Scripts use set -eu for error handling. Functions use snake_case naming. Readonly variables declared with 'readonly'. Script uses getopt for argument parsing.

setup_commands: microdnf -y install bash git && microdnf clean all

test_commands.notes: Syntax validation passed - no syntax errors
Syntax validation passed - no syntax errors
Help command works correctly (exits with code 2 as expected for usage display)
Docker build successful - image created and tagged
Container runs successfully and displays help text

container_recipe.notes: Full integration tests require GITHUB_TOKEN and access to actual GitHub repositories. Syntax validation and Docker build can be done without credentials. The test-entrypoint.bash script performs end-to-end tests of the sync functionality by interacting with real upstream and downstream GitHub repos.
